meta_title: For Students: project management to Solve no portfolio (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to project management and overcome no portfolio. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Project Management to Solve No Portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to build a world-class project management tool to overcome the 'no portfolio' trap. An intermediate guide using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: To overcome the 'no portfolio' hurdle, build a high-fidelity project management app. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative feature implementation, you demonstrate the ability to manage complex state, data persistence, and UI/UX—proving your engineering maturity to potential employers instantly.
content.intro:
The blank screen of a portfolio is the silent killer of junior developer careers. You have the theoretical knowledge, but employers aren't looking for tutorials; they are looking for evidence of architectural thinking. The 'no portfolio' problem isn't about a lack of code, but a lack of demonstrated impact.

Today, we bridge that gap. We aren't just building a To-Do list; we are engineering a robust Project Management system. By combining the rapid scaffolding of Antigravity with the autonomous precision of Claude Code, you will transform from a student into a builder who ships production-ready software.

steps.title: Define the Architectural Blueprint
steps.description: Establish the core data models for users, tasks, and project boards to ensure scalability from the start.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, scalable schema for a project management application including relational models for Users, Projects, Tasks, and Comments using TypeScript and Prisma.

steps.title: Bootstrap the Core API
steps.description: Create a type-safe backend that handles authentication and CRUD operations for your project data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a secure REST API with authentication middleware and endpoints for project task management.

steps.title: Implement Drag-and-Drop UX
steps.description: Build an interactive UI that allows users to move tasks across project boards, mimicking professional tools like Jira or Trello.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive React component using dnd-kit for a kanban board interface that updates task status in real-time.

steps.title: Integrate Real-time State Management
steps.description: Ensure the UI reflects backend changes immediately, providing a seamless 'pro-app' experience.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Set up TanStack Query for server state management to handle optimistic updates for task movements.

steps.title: Deployment and Documentation
steps.description: Finalize the build and document your architectural choices to create a compelling case study for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a professional README.md file highlighting the tech stack, design patterns, and features of this project management app.

pro_tips: Focus on 'Optimistic UI' updates; it's the single feature that makes a junior app feel like a senior-level product.
Always include a 'System Architecture' diagram in your README; it shows you think beyond just writing code.
Use Zod for schema validation on both client and server to prevent runtime errors that plague production apps.
Implement 'Soft Deletes' for tasks; it demonstrates an understanding of data integrity and production-grade safety.
Write your commit messages in the imperative mood to signal to recruiters that you understand professional Git workflows.
content.next_steps: Once your app is deployed, write a 300-word blog post explaining one technical challenge you overcame during the build. Link this post in your portfolio to provide context for your code.
content.conversion_block: You now have a production-grade project, a documented architecture, and a story to tell in interviews. Don't stop here. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
